title: No MSAL / Entra ID integration in frontend — auth token cannot be acquired or attached to API calls
body:
## Issue description

The web client (`client/`) has no Microsoft Authentication Library (MSAL) integration. Neither `@azure/msal-browser` nor `@azure/msal-react` appear in `client/package.json`. There is no `PublicClientApplication` configuration, no `MsalProvider`, no login redirect, no token acquisition, and no mechanism to attach a Bearer token to API requests.

Without MSAL the frontend cannot:
- Authenticate users with Microsoft Entra ID.
- Acquire access tokens for the Nova-Circle backend.
- Pass `Authorization: Bearer <token>` headers that the backend's `EntraTokenValidator` (`src/shared/auth/entra-token-validator.ts`) requires.

Every backend API call from a logged-in user would fail with `401 Unauthorized` even if a login screen existed, because there is no mechanism to obtain and attach a token.

## High-level requirements / Expected behavior

- Add `@azure/msal-browser` and `@azure/msal-react` as client production dependencies.
- Create an MSAL configuration module that reads `VITE_AZURE_CLIENT_ID`, `VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID`, and `VITE_AZURE_REDIRECT_URI` from Vite environment variables at build time. No values must be hard-coded.
- Wrap the React root in `MsalProvider` with the configured `PublicClientApplication` instance.
- Provide a central `useApiClient` hook (or equivalent) that:
  - Acquires a token silently via `acquireTokenSilent` before each request.
  - Falls back to `acquireTokenRedirect` or `acquireTokenPopup` when the silent call fails (e.g. expired session).
  - Attaches the acquired token as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on every `/api/v1/` request.
- Provide a logout action that calls `msalInstance.logoutRedirect()` and clears any locally cached state.
- Add `VITE_AZURE_CLIENT_ID`, `VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID`, and `VITE_AZURE_REDIRECT_URI` to `client/.env.example` (root `.env.example` should also be updated).
- The application must fail fast at initialisation if the required env vars are absent (throw a descriptive error before rendering).

## Additional notes

- The backend `EntraTokenValidator` validates the `oid`/`sub` claim and the `name`/`preferred_username` claim from the token. The MSAL scope must target the backend's registered application ID URI so the audience claim matches `AZURE_CLIENT_ID` on the server.
- During development and CI, the backend auth middleware accepts `X-Test-User-Id` and `X-Test-Display-Name` headers (when `NODE_ENV=test`) as a safe test bypass. Client-side MSAL integration tests should use MSAL's test utilities or a `PublicClientApplication` mock rather than real Entra calls.
- Token refresh must be handled transparently so users are not interrupted mid-session.
- MSAL configuration must be compatible with the Azure Container Apps / Static Web Apps deployment topology: the redirect URI must match the registered application's allowed redirect URIs in Entra ID.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `@azure/msal-browser` and `@azure/msal-react` are installed as client dependencies.
- [ ] A MSAL config module reads all settings from `VITE_*` environment variables; no hard-coded IDs or secrets.
- [ ] `MsalProvider` wraps the React app root.
- [ ] A central API client hook acquires a token silently and attaches it as a Bearer token on every `/api/v1/` request.
- [ ] Silent token acquisition failures trigger an interactive login flow.
- [ ] Logout calls `logoutRedirect()` and clears cached state.
- [ ] `client/.env.example` documents all required `VITE_AZURE_*` variables.
- [ ] The app throws a descriptive startup error when required env vars are absent.
- [ ] Unit tests mock `PublicClientApplication` and verify authenticated vs. unauthenticated API call behaviour.
- [ ] No real Entra ID calls are made in CI.
- [ ] CI passes.
